title: Missing slippage protection for batcher withdrawal in zapOut
reason: Final-output slippage is already user-bounded by `minAmountOut`; the reportable edge case is the separate stale-balance subsidy issue captured in F-005, not a generic lack of slippage protection.

title: No zero-address validation for governance in sweep
reason: This only turns a governance misconfiguration into governance self-harm; it is not a permissionless or protocol-level exploit in the zapper itself.

title: Missing event emissions for governance operations
reason: Observability issue only; no realistic fund-loss, theft, lockup, or DoS impact.

title: Unchecked call return values in zap function
reason: The low-level call success flag is checked, and swap success is further bounded by balance-delta plus `minAmountOut`; the meaningful risk from a no-op target is the persistent arbitrary approval issue already captured in F-001/F-003.

title: Floating pragma allows incompatible compiler versions
reason: Informational build-hygiene concern, not a concrete protocol-level vulnerability.

title: No access control on critical functions
reason: `sweep()` is explicitly restricted to `vault.governance()`; a compromised governance key is an out-of-scope trust assumption, not a missing access control bug.
